records.ety: [L. transitio: cf. F. transition. See Transient.]

defs: Passage from one place or state to another; charge; as, the transition of the weather from hot to cold.
quotes: There is no death, what seems so is transition.

defs: A direct or indirect passing from one key to another; a modulation.
A passing from one subject to another.
quotes: [He] with transition sweet, new speech resumes.

defs: Change from one form to another.
notes: ☞ This word is sometimes pronounced tran*sish"un; but according to Walker, Smart, and most other authorities, the customary and preferable pronunciation is tran*sizh"un, although this latter mode violates analogy. Other authorities say tran*zish"un.
